label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. So there are these five dancers: Alice, Bob, Claire, Dave, and Eve. They each have partners at the start of the song. The problem is about tracking the partner switches and figuring out who Bob ends up with at the end. The options are Lola, Karl, Patrick, Sam, or Helga. First, let me list out the initial pairings: - Alice is with Lola. - Bob is with Karl. - Claire is with Patrick. - Dave is with Sam. - Eve is with Helga. So the initial partners are: Alice - Lola Bob - Karl Claire - Patrick Dave - Sam Eve - Helga Now, the sequence of partner swaps is: 1. Dave and Alice switch partners. 2. Eve and Alice switch partners. 3. Bob and Dave switch partners. 4. Bob and Eve switch partners. 5. Bob and Claire switch partners. At the end of the dance, we need to find out who Bob is dancing with. Let me track each swap step by step. Maybe I can keep track of each person's partner after each swap. Starting with the initial pairings: Let me represent each person's current partner as a variable. Let's create a dictionary or a table. Initial: Alice: Lola Bob: Karl Claire: Patrick Dave: Sam Eve: Helga Let me process each swap one by one. First swap: Dave and Alice switch partners. So Dave's current partner is Sam, and Alice's current partner is Lola. After switching, Dave will have Lola, and Alice will have Sam. Wait, when two people switch partners, they exchange their partners. So if Dave is dancing with Sam, and Alice is dancing with Lola, after switching, Dave will be dancing with Lola, and Alice will be dancing with Sam. So after first swap: Alice: Sam Dave: Lola Others remain same: Bob: Karl Claire: Patrick Eve: Helga Second swap: Eve and Alice switch partners. Eve's current partner is Helga; Alice's current partner is Sam. After swapping, Eve will have Sam, and Alice will have Helga. So after second swap: Alice: Helga Eve: Sam Others: Bob: Karl Claire: Patrick Dave: Lola Third swap: Bob and Dave switch partners. Bob's current partner is Karl; Dave's current partner is Lola. After swapping, Bob will have Lola, and Dave will have Karl. After third swap: Bob: Lola Dave: Karl Others: Alice: Helga Claire: Patrick Eve: Sam Fourth swap: Bob and Eve switch partners. Bob's current partner is Lola; Eve's current partner is Sam. After swapping, Bob will have Sam, and Eve will have Lola. After fourth swap: Bob: Sam Eve: Lola Others: Alice: Helga Claire: Patrick Dave: Karl Fifth swap: Bob and Claire switch partners. Bob's current partner is Sam; Claire's current partner is Patrick. After swapping, Bob will have Patrick, and Claire will have Sam. After fifth swap: Bob: Patrick Claire: Sam Others: Alice: Helga Dave: Karl Eve: Lola So at the end of the dance, Bob is dancing with Patrick. Looking at the options: (C) Patrick is option C. Let me verify my steps again to make sure I didn't make any mistake. First swap: Dave and Alice switch partners. So Alice (Lola) and Dave (Sam) → Alice: Sam, Dave: Lola. Correct. Second swap: Eve and Alice switch partners. Alice (Sam) and Eve (Helga) → Alice: Helga, Eve: Sam. Correct. Third swap: Bob and Dave switch partners. Bob (Karl) and Dave (Lola) → Bob: Lola, Dave: Karl. Correct. Fourth swap: Bob and Eve switch partners. Bob (Lola)
